title: Ateramnus integer
taxonomy: Ateramnus integer is classified within the family Malvaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family. The genus Ateramnus, to which Ateramnus integer belongs, is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Australia and Asia. The plant is further classified as a species within the Ateramnus genus. Ateramnus integer is sometimes referred to as the small bugweed or small blue bush, and is often used as a groundcover or ornamental plant. Its classification is also closely related to the family's other members, including the hibiscus and okra.

intro: Ateramnus integer, also known as Mulga or Mulga Bush, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and narrow leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in indigenous Australian medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.
